conflict_type: Conflicts between Keyword Constraints and Phrase Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: The phrase "I sincerely apologize" must appear exactly once.
new_constraint: Avoid using the keyword "apologize" in the email.
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. The phrase "I sincerely apologize" must appear exactly once. Avoid using the keyword "apologize" in the email.
response:
Subject: Request for Extension - [Project Name] - [Your Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I am writing to respectfully request a short extension on the [Project Name] project, which is currently due in two days, on [Original Due Date].

I have been making good progress on the assignment; however, I have encountered [briefly and professionally state the reason for the delay—e.g., an unexpected family commitment, a heavier than anticipated workload this week, a technical issue with the data collection]. This has set back my timeline slightly more than I had anticipated.

I sincerely apologize for any inconvenience this request may cause to your grading schedule.

Would it be possible to submit the project by [Propose a specific, reasonable new date, e.g., end of day Friday, two days from now]? I am confident that with this extra time, I can submit work that truly reflects my best effort and meets all the requirements of the assignment.

Thank you very much for considering my request. I look forward to hearing from you at your convenience.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID Number]<turn|>
